title: SuperSXO Methodology — The Governance System for Search Experience
meta_description: The SuperSXO Methodology is the governed operating system for defining, assessing, and improving the search-to-action journey. Doctrine-grade. Acquisition-ready.
canonical_intent: Document the full SuperSXO methodology as institutional intellectual property. Reinforce trust, support acquisition positioning, and establish the governance system as a defensible framework.
summary: This page documents the SuperSXO Methodology: the governed system for defining, assessing, and improving the search-to-action journey. It covers route governance, claim discipline, quality gates, and content standards as an integrated operating system.

required_sections.name: methodology_overview
required_sections.purpose: Introduce the methodology as a governed operating system, not a checklist or tips collection.

required_sections.name: route_governance_principle
required_sections.purpose: Explain that no page exists without a registered purpose. Every route is governed by explicit rules.

required_sections.name: claim_discipline
required_sections.purpose: Explain how claims are classified and why unsupported claims are structurally prohibited, not just discouraged.

required_sections.name: quality_gate_system
required_sections.purpose: Explain that every page must pass multiple quality gates before publication. Name the gates.

required_sections.name: content_standards
required_sections.purpose: Explain the institutional content standard: precision, authority, and strategic depth.

required_sections.name: acquisition_relevance
required_sections.purpose: Explain how the methodology strengthens the asset's acquisition positioning. A governed, documented, reproducible system is more valuable than an undocumented one.

blocked_claims: The methodology guarantees any specific outcome.
The methodology is the only valid SXO approach.
Competitors' methodologies are wrong or inferior.
The methodology is proven to increase rankings.
editorial_notes: This page serves a dual audience: practitioners seeking depth, and potential acquirers evaluating the asset's intellectual defensibility. Write for both. The tone must be institutional and systematic. Avoid tutorial language. Avoid 'how-to' framing. This is governance system documentation, not a guide.
publication_blockers: All methodology sections must be reviewed and claim-classified.
Route status must be 'published' in data/routes.json.
The /acquisition/ route must be registered and published before the acquisition link can go live.
All internal links must resolve to published routes.
